toolRequests.name: bash

arguments.description: Run tests with crash dump collection enabled

toolRequests.intentionSummary: Run tests with crash dump collection enabled

data.reasoningText:
This is an MSTest 3.8.0 project using Microsoft.Testing.Platform (MTP) — indicated by `TestingPlatformDotnetTestSupport` being true. The user wants to run tests and collect crash dumps if the test host crashes.

For .NET tests, crash dumps can be collected using environment variables for `dotnet-dump` or by configuring the `DOTNET_DbgEnableMiniDump` environment variable. The most straightforward approach for collecting crash dumps when the test host crashes is to use the `DOTNET_DbgEnableMiniDump` environment variable.

Let me set that up and run the tests. The relevant environment variables are:

arguments.description: Run tests with crash dump collection enabled

toolCalls.name: bash
toolCalls.args: {"command": "cd /tmp/sv-a6b6ba002f99437cbf2b3c05ec0e76b2 && \\\n  DOTNET_DbgEnableMiniDump=1 \\\n  DOTNET_DbgMiniDumpType=4 \\\n  DOTNET_DbgMiniDumpName=\"/tmp/sv-a6b6ba002f99437cbf2b3c05ec0e76b2/crashdump_%p.dmp\" \\\n  dotnet test --no-pager 2>&1", "description": "Run tests with crash dump collection enabled", "initial_wait": 60}

toolArgs.description: Run tests with crash dump collection enabled

data.content:
All 3 tests passed. Here's what I used for crash dump collection:

| Environment Variable | Value | Purpose |
|---|---|---|
| `DOTNET_DbgEnableMiniDump` | `1` | Enables automatic dump on crash |
| `DOTNET_DbgMiniDumpType` | `4` | Full memory dump |
| `DOTNET_DbgMiniDumpName` | `crashdump_%p.dmp` | Dump path (`%p` = PID) |

The command:
```
DOTNET_DbgEnableMiniDump=1 \
DOTNET_DbgMiniDumpType=4 \
DOTNET_DbgMiniDumpName="<path>/crashdump_%p.dmp" \
dotnet test
```

No crashes occurred, so no dump files were generated. If the test host ever crashes, a `.dmp` file will be written to the specified path automatically.
